channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Pope Francis hospitalized to treat bronchitis, undergo tests as Holy Year events canceled
item.description: Pope Francis was hospitalized Friday to treat a weeklong bout of bronchitis and undergo diagnostic tests, the Vatican said, confirming the latest issues with the 88-year-old's pontiff's health that forced him to cancel his agenda through Monday at least.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas death row inmate mouths final 2-word message to victims' families before execution
item.description: A remorseful death row inmate pleaded for forgiveness and mothed one final message before being put to death in Texas Thursday, 20 years after he killed his strip club manager and another man.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida newlyweds get justice after family fishing trip ended in murder
item.description: A Florida killer found guilty of killing a newlywed couple in front of their toddler was finally executed on Thursday, nearly three decades after his gruesome crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Issa Rae cancels sold-out Kennedy Center after Trump takeover
item.description: "Unfortunately, due to what I believe to be an infringement on the values of an institution that has faithfully celebrated artists of all backgrounds through all mediums, I've decided to cancel my appearance at this venue," the Emmy-nominated "Insecure" actor said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KTLA 5

item.title: Man accused of shooting Kansas City teen Ralph Yarl to change plea
item.description: Andrew Lester, the man accused of shooting Ralph Yarl, a Kansas City teen in 2023, will take a plea deal in a last-minute hearing on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge pauses President Trump's order restricting gender-affirming care for trans youth
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s recent executive order aimed at restricting gender-affirming health care for transgender people under age 19.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Humpback whale briefly swallows kayaker
item.description: A humpback whale briefly swallowed a kayaker off Chilean Patagonia before quickly releasing him unharmed. The incident, caught on camera, quickly went viral.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NYC mayor to allow immigration officials to operate at Rikers jail after Trump border czar meeting
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams says he’ll allow federal immigration officials to operate at the city’s Rikers Island jail following a meeting Thursday with President Donald Trump’s border czar. Adams said he’ll issue an executive order reestablishing U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What's an atmospheric river? A pineapple express? AP explains the weather phenomenon
item.description: Atmospheric rivers are a plume of moisture that extend across the Pacific to near Hawaii. When they hit land they can cause heavy rains and snow.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oklahoma governor says he'll stop plan to collect kids' immigration status
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Oklahoma Gov. Kevin Stitt said he will stop a plan pushed by the state’s top education official to collect the immigration status of children when they register for public school.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Denny's to close dozens more restaurants in 2025
item.description: Denny's was already planning to close dozens of locations this year. Now it's planning to close even more.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Watch live: House panel convenes hearing on USAID amid DOGE overhaul
item.description: The House Foreign Affairs Committee is holding a hearing Thursday morning looking into what GOP leadership has called the "betrayal" of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID).
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Kamala Harris would be Democratic frontrunner for California governor in 2026, poll suggests
item.description: Former Vice President Harris would be the Democratic frontrunner for governor of California if she jumped into the Golden State’s 2026 race to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom (D), new polling shows.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KRON4

item.title: Live updates: Trump, Modi talk trade and tariffs; Senate confirms Kennedy to lead HHS
item.description: President Trump welcomes his second global leader this week with a visit from Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: Dog seen riding in unsecured kennel along Indiana expressway now recovering
item.description: Video showed the dog in a kennel on a trailer that was being towed along an Indiana expressway in frigid temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KRON4

item.title: Duolingo owl dead, killed by Cybertruck, company says
item.description: (KRON) -- In what some are calling a "master class" in killing off a company mascot, language-learning app Duolingo announced Tuesday that its trademark mascot, "Duo," aka "The Duolingo Owl," is dead.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: More than 500 JOANN stores expected to close, including some in San Diego County
item.description: When JOANN filed for bankruptcy last month, the company said it expected all of its stores to remain open.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Could obesity drugs help with alcohol cravings?: new study
item.description: Medications that have transformed the treatment of obesity may also help people drink less alcohol, according to new government-funded research.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Military plane crashes near San Diego: authorities
item.description: Emergency crews are responding to reports of a military plane that crashed into the San Diego Bay near Shelter Island, according to the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KRON4

item.title: Baltimore sues Glock over handguns that can easily be converted to automatic weapons
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Baltimore is suing gunmaker Glock over design features that allow its handguns to be easily modified to become fully automatic weapons, which enables criminals to inflict more damage and makes the city more dangerous, attorneys argue in a complaint filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds singer R. Kelly's convictions, 30-year prison term
item.description: R. Kelly’s racketeering and sex trafficking convictions, along with a 30-year prison sentence, were upheld Wednesday by a federal appeals court that concluded the singer exploited his fame for over a quarter century to sexually abuse girls and young women.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Government watchdogs fired by Trump sue his administration and ask a judge to reinstate them
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Eight government watchdogs have sued over their mass firing that removed oversight of President Donald Trump's new administration.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI says it found 2,400 new JFK assassination records
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The FBI on Tuesday said it discovered 2,400 new records related to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy as federal agencies work to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order last month to release thousands of files.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California city passes controversial homeless camping ban
item.description: The ordinance, which allows the city to fine those who help the homeless and makes it illegal for homeless people to camp on public property, was approved in a 6-1 vote after more than two hours of public comments.
item.dc:creator: Joey Horta, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: School gym roof collapses under 350 tons of snow
item.description: A recent snowstorm that pummeled parts of Oregon caused the roof at a high school gym to collapse under the weight of about 700,000 pounds of snow, school officials said.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, Fox 5

item.title: Italy prepares scenic, historic Olympic venues with games now one year away
item.description: This marks the first time Milan will host the Winter Olympics, with the opening ceremony scheduled at San Siro Stadium. The games will feature events in multiple locations, including hockey in a new arena in Milan and various alpine events in Cortina.
item.dc:creator: Matt Barnes, KRON4

item.title: Want to send video of elephant pooping to your ex for V-day? Here's how
item.description: Want to "name a turd after a turd" for Valentine's Day? The Memphis Zoo has you covered by sending a video to your ex or your least favorite person.
item.dc:creator: Deja Davis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Audio from Titan submersible implosion released by NOAA
item.description: In the 23-second recording, the sound of static can be heard, followed by a booming noise — then more static.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KRON4

item.title: Ozzy Osbourne's 'final show' with original Black Sabbath: What we know
item.description: The performance, called "The Back to the Beginning," will cap off an all-day event featuring sets from several iconic heavy metal acts.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Republican lawmakers urge Trump to respect federal court rulings
item.description: GOP lawmakers are growing increasingly worried about the nation plunging into a constitutional crisis if Trump ignores court orders halting his most aggressive actions.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4

item.title: Disney, amid changes to DEI initiatives, will alter content warning ahead of 'Peter Pan,' 'Dumbo' and other older films
item.description: Disney has become the latest company to change its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) initiatives as pressure from the federal government to eliminate such programs continues.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: Jack the Ripper's identity revealed after DNA match, historian says
item.description: A historian believes he has determined Jack the Ripper's identity through a DNA match of a shawl found at the scene of one of his murders.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 14 bands and artists nominated for Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
item.description: Mariah Carey, Chubby Checker, Cyndi Lauper, OutKast and Phish are some the 2025 nominees for induction into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, a list that also includes two sets of musical brothers who have had public feuds and recent reunions — the Black Crowes and Oasis.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump doubles down on plan to empty Gaza. This is what he has said and what's at stake
item.description: Behind U.S. President Donald Trump’s vows to turn Gaza into a “Riviera of the Middle East” lies a plan to forcibly drive a population from its land, rights groups say, warning it could be a war crime under international law.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Musk appears at White House defending DOGE's work but acknowledging mistakes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's most powerful adviser, Elon Musk, made a rare public appearance at the White House on Tuesday to defend the swift and extensive cuts he's pushing across the federal government while acknowledging there have been mistakes and will be more.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Court sets March 19 date for first execution in Arizona in over 2 years
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The Arizona Supreme Court set a March 19 execution date Tuesday for a man who pleaded guilty to murder more than 17 years ago and recently said his death sentence was “long overdue.” It would be the state’s first application of the death penalty in more than two years.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs executive order restoring plastic straw use in government
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing the federal government to return to using plastic straws.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC
